rationale: The citing paper uses Hamilton and Chernev (2010) in the conceptual background to define and contrast browsing versus buying goals, including direct quoted distinctions about information gathering versus immediate purchase use. That stage-goal distinction is then carried into the citing paper's core informational versus transactional framing and hypothesis development about construal in search. Although cited in a limited number of places, it plays a meaningful conceptual role rather than a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Hamilton and Chernev (2010) as conceptual grounding for the idea that consumers in browsing and buying stages have different goals and temporal proximity to purchase. It uses that distinction to motivate its own informational-versus-transactional decision-journey framework in online search.
